# ipmi_power (Resource)

Declaratively manage BMC chassis power state. Reconciles drift on each refresh — if the host gets powered off out-of-band, the next apply turns it back on.

Only steady states (`on`, `off`) are supported. Imperative actions like `cycle` and `reset` will be a separate `ipmi_power_action` resource in v0.2.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `state` (String) Desired power state: `on` or `off`.

### Optional

- `cipher_suite` (Number)
- `host` (String)
- `interface` (String)
- `password` (String, Sensitive)
- `port` (Number)
- `power_off_on_destroy` (Boolean) If true, `terraform destroy` powers the host off. Default false (destroy leaves the host running).
- `username` (String)

### Read-Only

- `current_state` (String) Observed chassis power state at last read.
- `id` (String) Resource ID — `<host>:<port>` of the managed BMC.
- `last_updated` (String) RFC3339 timestamp of the last SetPowerState call.
